subject: Subtomo reconstruction in Warp without progress
body:
Hi all,

after some really impressive Warp/M benchmarking with 70S ribosomes I'm
currently struggling to apply the tomo pipeline on a rather small
membrane-bound protein.

I have a couple of tomos with membranes and surface-bound proteins that
were collected in focus with VPP. Tomo reconstruction with binning to
8Ang was successful, as well as template matching with a cutout from one
of the tomograms at the same pixel size (plus spherical mask). Template
matching .star files look good, with FOM values ranging from 28 to 14,
the _corr.mrc files show strong white values for membranes plus attached
proteins. However, "Export sub-tomograms" does just nothing (waited for
days, subtomo folder still empty, CPU/MEM/GPU inactive)

One input file per tilt series checked, normalized coordinates, 4 Ang
scaled pixel size, Box size 32px with 100 Ang particle diameter. ,
checked "Volumes"; No Shift, no pre-rotation, no limit to first 1 tilts
by dose. Yes to all other options below.

WarpWorker.exe Window last output:

Received "LoadGainRef", with 5 arguments, for GPU #0 6713 MB free

Set headerless parameters to 7676, 7420, 0, int8

0.000

Loaded gain reference and defect map: , False, False, False,

0.000

Received "TomoExportParticles", with 4 arguments, for GPU #0, 6713 MB free

---

 From this stage all Warp processes are inactive on the CPU (1%), take
up about 10 GB of RAM that doesn't change, 0.1% GPU load, not more than
1 Mbps network (that's were the tomos come from).

Progress window doesn't change.

Any ideas?

Best wishes from Germany,

Dan




------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Forschungszentrum Juelich GmbH
52425 Juelich
Sitz der Gesellschaft: Juelich
Eingetragen im Handelsregister des Amtsgerichts Dueren Nr. HR B 3498
Vorsitzender des Aufsichtsrats: MinDir Volker Rieke
Geschaeftsfuehrung: Prof. Dr.-Ing. Wolfgang Marquardt (Vorsitzender),
Karsten Beneke (stellv. Vorsitzender), Prof. Dr.-Ing. Harald Bolt
